content:
- Full gates passed: make check, make lint, make test.
- Updated the task and plan files to done/true and ticked the executed slices; ran .ralph/task_switch.sh successfully.
- Next: review final git status, commit all tracked and untracked files with the required task-finished message including test evidence, push, then stop.

- Read prior progress and current/current_done task summaries.
- Confirmed story-03 task-01 is already done and active backlog currently starts at story-03 task-02, with all later tasks blocked by chain dependencies.
- Next: refresh Ralph summaries, verify chooser rules file, inspect repo stack/architecture, then write the selected task path to .ralph/current_task.txt.

- Refreshed Ralph task summaries and verified chooser rules file matches the operating instructions.
- Confirmed actual codebase stack is Go 1.25 with a cmd entrypoint, internal app/config/database/httpserver/benchmark modules, and make targets for lint(vet+gofmt)/test.
- Next: re-read refreshed active tasks, inspect the next unblocked story task definition, then write that exact path to .ralph/current_task.txt and stop.

- Re-read refreshed active tasks and confirmed there are no active bug tasks and only one unblocked failing task.
- Selected .ralph/tasks/story-03-core-benchmark/task-02-core-read-write-transaction-workloads.md because its blocker is done and every later task remains blocked behind it.
- Next: start task-02 with the tdd skill, implement database/sql benchmark workloads, and keep make check/test/lint green.

- Read prior progress, current task pointer, and the tdd/improve-code-boundaries skill instructions.
- Confirmed the active task is .ralph/tasks/story-03-core-benchmark/task-02-core-read-write-transaction-workloads.md and I need to inspect whether it is still in planning or ready for execution.
- Next: read the task file, find any plan file referenced there, and decide whether this turn must stop after planning changes or can proceed into TDD execution.

- Read the active task, existing story-03 plan style, current benchrunner/coordinator/app code, and the relevant tdd/improve-code-boundaries references.
- Confirmed task-02 is planning-only this turn because the task is still not started; current runner only performs schema setup and then waits on context cancellation.
- Confirmed there is no stats aggregation module yet (story-04 adds it), so the plan should keep task-02 focused on real workload execution and a clean future hook rather than inventing placeholder stats DTOs now.
- Next: write the dated task plan under the story-03 task-02 plans directory, link it from the task file, promote it to NOW EXECUTE if the interface is coherent, and then stop immediately.

- Created and linked the task-02 plan at .ralph/tasks/story-03-core-benchmark/task-02-core-read-write-transaction-workloads_plans/2026-04-30-core-read-write-transaction-workloads-plan.md.
- Promoted the plan to NOW EXECUTE after resolving the main design ambiguity: keep public profiles unchanged, implement point/range under read and insert/update under write, and fail join/lock visibly until story-06.
- The plan explicitly calls for a benchrunner boundary cleanup: replace the current fake live run with a real worker/session-owned database/sql workload runner that keeps pacing, warmup, and workload selection private to internal/benchrunner.
- Next: on the next turn, execute the plan with strict vertical TDD slices, update the task acceptance boxes as slices land, and only finish when make check, make lint, and make test all pass.

- Re-read Ralph startup state, confirmed active task is story-03 task-02, and verified the linked plan is in NOW EXECUTE state.
- Re-read the required tdd and improve-code-boundaries skills and the task acceptance criteria before touching code.
- Next: inspect internal/benchrunner and coordinator code, then execute the plan in vertical TDD slices with frequent progress updates.

- Replaced the old benchrunner string-based tests with behavior tests that drive the runner through database/sql.
- First slice exposed the real product gap: benchrunner still only sets up schema and blocks forever, so the read workload test hangs until timed out.
- Next: rewrite internal/benchrunner into a real worker-runner with private workload selection and lifecycle control, then rerun the narrow red slice.

- Implemented a real internal/benchrunner worker runner with private workload selection, pacing, client resizing, duration stop, and database/sql execution.
- Added behavior tests for read/write/mixed/transaction profiles, deterministic runtime controls, and coordinator-visible workload failure propagation.
- Next: gofmt the changed files, run the full project gates (make check, make lint, make test), then update the Ralph task/plan metadata if everything stays green.
